---
title: "Using Spatial Lag and Regression to Predict Income Inequality"
description: "A project that used GeoDa, ArcGis Pro and R with a variety of techniques to predict income inequality within New York City."
author: "Robert Mullins"
date: 2025-03-17
image: "/images/inequality.jpg"

categories: [Data Analytics, GeoDa, R, ArcGis Pro]
---

## Overview

This report models the Gini index across New York City census tracts using OLS, spatial lag, spatial error, and geographically weighted regression to account for spatial autocorrelation. GWR performed best overall, explaining 57% of inequality variation with no residual spatial autocorrelation, outperforming all other models.

::: {.callout-note}
This project was completed as part of "Spatial Econometrics and Data" at Waseda University, Japan. 
:::

## Read the full report

[{{< fa file-pdf >}} Read Full Report](/projects/reports/inequality_predict.pdf){.btn .btn-outline-primary target="_blank"}

<iframe src="/projects/reports/inequality_predict.pdf" 
        width="100%" 
        height="800px"
        style="border: none; border-radius: 8px;">
</iframe>

---
